subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/war rebuilt from independent sources (V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House, HRW + Meduza, Novaya Gazeta Europe, iStories, The Bell, Mediazona + CSIS/ISW/Bank of Finland BOFIT); Russian state media (TASS/RT/RIA/Rossiya-1) and Kremlin/Rosstat governance claims EXCLUDED there.
Rosstat data is heavily politicised in wartime and treated as low-confidence/disputed; demographic data has been state-classified since 2025. Scores reflect observable actions, not state messaging.

Standout drags: the aggressor in an ongoing war, a sanctioned war-economy, near-total repression and press control, and a demographic crisis. Overall outcome distribution: negative (fat-tail downside).

economic.notes: Low headline debt, undercut by a sanctioned war-economy draining its sovereign fund, a fragile banking sector, and a discounted petrostate export model.

fiscal_state.notes: Low public debt (~20% GDP) but a war that consumes a huge share of the budget and is draining the sovereign-wealth fund toward depletion - war-economy unsustainability. (Moscow Times; OSW; BOFIT)

monetary_independence.notes: The central bank is technically competent (rates cut to ~14.5% as inflation eased) but operates under FX controls and sanctions - competent but constrained. (CBR; Moscow Times)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The 2022 reserve freeze exposed a weaponizable vulnerability; the gold/alternative-payment pivot is defensive. (Brookings)

inflation.notes: Officially easing (~5.7%) but war-driven and politicised in the underlying data - scored worse than the headline. (CBR; BOFIT)

banking_stability.notes: NPLs above the systemic threshold, opaque defence-sector lending, and think-tank warnings of a systemic crisis. (Moscow Times; bne IntelliNews)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A petrostate rerouted to China/India at steep discounts with oil/gas revenue sharply down - a classic sanctioned rentier/resource-curse dynamic. (BOFIT; EU Council)

institutional.notes: A capable coercive apparatus atop no judicial independence, politicised statistics, and near-total press control.

rule_of_law.notes: No judicial independence, foreign-firm asset seizures, and pervasive repression. (Freedom House 2026; V-Dem)

statistical_integrity.notes: Official statistics are politicised, with demographic data classified since 2025. (Moscow Times; Carnegie)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable coercive state apparatus, undercut by pervasive defence-budget theft. (Freedom House; TI)

press_freedom.notes: Near-total control: independent media exiled or banned and dozens of journalists jailed. (RSF 2026; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Suppressed stability with a fat-tail succession risk, severe repression, and a demographic crisis.

political_stability.notes: Entrenched personalist rule - managed/suppressed stability with a fat-tail succession/coup risk (recall the 2023 mutiny). (Freedom House; ISW)

civil_liberties.notes: Severe repression, with multi-year sentences for anti-war speech. (Freedom House; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Residence routes exist, but it is a mobilization state with exit risk for men - a deep risk ceiling. (HRW; Meduza)

social_cohesion.notes: War propaganda, mounting casualties, and the emigration of the educated. (Carnegie)

demographics.notes: A pre-war decline (record-low births, deep natural decrease) compounded by war deaths and brain drain - a crisis. (Moscow Times; Carnegie; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A heavily sanctioned Western pariah and the aggressor in an ongoing war.

alliance_reliability.notes: A pariah to the West, aligned with China/Iran/North Korea in dependency-prone, junior relationships. (ISW; CSIS)

conflict_involvement.notes: The aggressor in the ongoing Ukraine war, with 2026 ceasefire attempts collapsed and talks stagnated. (Al Jazeera; Reuters)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: The most-sanctioned major economy, under heavy capital controls. (EU Council)

physical_practical.notes: War-strained healthcare, uneven infrastructure, and rising crime, with mixed climate exposure.

climate.notes: Two-sided - a near-term high-latitude/Arctic-access window, but permafrost-thaw infrastructure collapse, Siberian wildfire, and methane feedbacks plus weak readiness turn it net-negative in longer outlooks. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN; Burke et al.)

healthcare.notes: War-strained and underfunded outside the major cities, with a large untreated-veteran burden. (BBC Russian; Meduza)

infrastructure.notes: Uneven Soviet-legacy infrastructure with war-diverted investment. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: Rising crime at multi-year highs, linked partly to returning veterans, with a drained police force. (Moscow Times; Verstka)

summary: A heavily sanctioned war-economy: low headline public debt masks a budget dominated by war spending that is draining the sovereign-wealth fund, a fragile and opaque banking sector, and a petrostate export model rerouted to China and India at steep discounts. Institutions offer a capable coercive apparatus but no judicial independence, politicised statistics, and near-total press control. The country is the aggressor in an ongoing war, a Western pariah under the heaviest sanctions of any major economy, and faces severe repression and a deepening demographic crisis. The dominant risk is fat-tail downside.
